question: [File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with _quoteTokenConfigurations[inputToken].enabled check] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER call swapExactInput with inputToken set to a quote token that has enabled=1 but is also a launched token (i.e. both a quote token and a bonding-curve token), under the precondition that _getSwapImplementation checks _quoteTokenConfigurations[inputToken].enabled to determine if it's a buy, causing the router to treat the launched token as a quote token and route to the wrong implementation, violating the invariant that inputToken and outputToken must be distinct and one must be a valid quote token, causing scoped impact: attacker exploits routing confusion to buy tokens at an incorrect price or extract reserve? Local proof idea: unit test with inputToken == outputToken == a token that is both a quote token and a launched token; assert SameToken revert.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (sell path) with _burnToken for TOKEN_TAXED and tax splitter callback] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER sell a TOKEN_TAXED token where the tax splitter's receive() function re-enters swapExactInput to sell more tokens, under the precondition that _burnToken calls the tax splitter before updating the reserve, causing the re-entrant sell to use the pre-sell reserve (higher price), violating the invariant that reserve must be updated before any external calls that could trigger re-entry, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts excess BNB by selling at the pre-sell price via re-entrant tax splitter callback? Local proof idea: unit test with a mock tax splitter that re-enters sell in its receive(); assert the re-entrant sell uses the post-sell reserve.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with dexThreshType._1_PERCENT and large single buy] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buy a token with DexThreshType._1_PERCENT in a single transaction that pushes circulatingSupply past dexSupplyThresh, under the precondition that the migration is triggered within the same buy transaction and the attacker's tokens are minted before migration removes the reserve, causing the attacker to hold tokens that are immediately worth more at the DEX price, violating the invariant that the last buyer before migration must not receive tokens at a price below the DEX listing price, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts the price difference between bonding curve and DEX listing price? Local proof idea: fork-test with _1_PERCENT threshold; buy to trigger migration in one tx; compare token value at DEX vs cost on bonding curve.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with non-18-decimal quote token and estimateSupplyV2 scaleFactor overflow] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buy tokens with a 6-decimal quote token (e.g. USDC) where reserve is near type(uint128).max / scaleFactor, under the precondition that scaledReserve = reserve * 10^12 overflows uint256 in estimateSupplyV2, causing the supply estimate to be incorrect (revert or wrap), violating the invariant that estimateSupplyV2 must not overflow for any valid reserve value within uint128 range, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers a revert that blocks all buys (DoS) — REJECT unless overflow produces a non-reverting incorrect supply that allows excess token minting? Local proof idea: fuzz test estimateSupplyV2 with reserveDecimals=6 and reserve near type(uint128).max / 10^12; assert no silent overflow.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (sell path) with estimateReserveV2 rounding up for 6-decimal quote] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER sell tokens for a 6-decimal quote token where estimateReserveV2 rounds up the scaled reserve then rounds up again in the scaleFactor division, under the precondition that double rounding-up causes the returned reserve to exceed the actual quote tokens held by Portal, causing Portal to transfer more USDC to the seller than it holds, violating the invariant that estimateReserveV2(supply, 6) <= actual USDC balance attributable to this token, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts more USDC than deposited into the bonding curve? Local proof idea: differential fuzz test: for any supply, assert estimateReserveV2(supply, 6) * 10^12 <= estimateReserve(supply) + 1 (at most 1 wei of 18-decimal rounding).

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with TOKEN_V3_PERMIT and TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buy a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token on the bonding curve where TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE (60%) of the collected fee is sent to the protocol, under the precondition that the remaining 40% is sent to the token beneficiary (the attacker), causing the attacker to receive 40% of 1.25% = 0.5% of every buy as an immediate reward, violating the invariant that bonding-curve fee distribution must match the documented split and not allow the beneficiary to extract more than their entitled share, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts 0.5% of all buy volume as bonding-curve fee rewards by setting themselves as beneficiary? Local proof idea: fork-test launching a V7 TOKEN_V3_PERMIT with attacker as beneficiary; have victims buy; assert attacker receives exactly 40% of the 1.25% fee.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (sell path) with TOKEN_TAXED_V3 and TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE post-migration] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER who is the commissionReceiver of a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 token trigger collectV4Fees after migration, under the precondition that TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE (41%) of LP fees go to the protocol and the remaining 59% is distributed via TaxProcessorV2 to mktAddr/dividend/deflation/LP, and the commissionReceiver receives a commission from the mktBps portion, causing the attacker to receive a perpetual commission from LP fees, violating the invariant that commission extraction from LP fees must be bounded by the documented commission schedule, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts commission from LP fees beyond valid entitlement? Local proof idea: fork-test with a V7 TOKEN_TAXED_V3 post-migration; call collectV4Fees; assert commissionReceiver receives exactly _commissionForTax() fraction of the mktBps portion.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with CURVE_RH_BNB_1X5_32BNB and h=3359591794 ether] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buy tokens on the CURVE_RH_BNB_1X5_32BNB curve where h=3359591794 ether is a very large virtual token reserve, under the precondition that estimateSupply computes TOTAL_SUPPLY + h - k/(r+eth) and with large h the result can exceed TOTAL_SUPPLY (1e9 ether) for small eth values, causing supply to be computed as a value larger than TOTAL_SUPPLY, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply must never exceed TOTAL_SUPPLY, causing scoped impact: attacker mints tokens beyond maxSupply by exploiting the large h parameter? Local proof idea: fuzz test estimateSupply with CURVE_RH_BNB_1X5_32BNB parameters and small reserve values; assert supply <= TOTAL_SUPPLY.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (sell path) with TOKEN_V2_PERMIT and _eraseToken] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER trigger _eraseToken for a TOKEN_V2_PERMIT token (which transfers tokens to the dead address without changing circulatingSupply), under the precondition that _eraseToken is called during migration to burn excess tokens, and if the erased amount is incorrectly computed, causing circulatingSupply to be higher than actual tokens in circulation, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply must equal actual tokens held by non-dead addresses, causing scoped impact: attacker exploits inflated circulatingSupply to sell tokens at a lower price than the curve supports, extracting excess BNB? Local proof idea: unit test _eraseToken; assert Portal's token balance decreases by exactly the erased amount and circulatingSupply is unchanged.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (buy path) with quoteToken == address(0) but msg.value == 0] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER call swapExactInput with inputToken == address(0) (native BNB buy) and inputAmount > 0 but msg.value == 0, under the precondition that the bonding curve implementation does not validate msg.value >= inputAmount before crediting the reserve, causing the reserve to be incremented by inputAmount without receiving any BNB, violating the invariant that reserve increase must equal actual BNB received (msg.value), causing scoped impact: attacker mints tokens for free by crediting a reserve that was never funded? Local proof idea: unit test swapExactInput with inputToken=address(0), inputAmount=1 ether, msg.value=0; assert it reverts with InsufficientEth.

[File: 'src/Portal.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access'] [Function: swapExactInput (sell path) with TOKEN_TAXED_V3 and antiFarmerDuration active] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER sell a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 token during the antiFarmer period (PoolState.EnforcedAntiFarmer) via swapExactInput on the bonding curve (not DEX), under the precondition that the antiFarmer restriction only applies to DEX pool transfers and not to bonding curve sells, causing the attacker to sell tokens at the bonding curve price without paying the anti-farmer tax, violating the invariant that anti-farmer restrictions must apply to all sell paths during the enforcement period, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts BNB by selling at bonding curve price while anti-farmer tax is active on DEX? Local proof idea: fork-test with a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 in EnforcedAntiFarmer state; call swapExactInput sell; assert anti-farmer tax is applied or the sell is blocked.
